sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचअपरं भवतो जन्म परं जन्म विवस्वतः । कथमेतद्विजानीयां त्वमादौ प्रोक्तवानिति ॥
hindiTranslation: अर्जुन बोले  आपका जन्म तो अभीका है और सूर्यका जन्म बहुत पुराना है अतः आपने ही सृष्टिके आदिमें सूर्यसे यह योग कहा था  यह बात मैं कैसे समझूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Arjuna said: Your birth is recent, while the birth of the Sun is ancient. How am I to understand that you taught this yoga at the beginning of creation?
essence: Doubt becomes the doorway to deeper seeing.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna doubts Krishna's claim. Krishna is younger than the Sun, so Arjuna cannot see how Krishna could have taught this yoga at the beginning of creation.
simpleInsight: Honest doubt can open the door to deeper understanding.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna has just heard Krishna say this ancient yoga was first taught at the start of creation. That claim collides with ordinary time: Krishna stands before him as a living charioteer, while the Sun is unimaginably ancient. Arjuna asks how both can be true.
modernRelevance: You hear a claim that sounds impossible from someone standing right in front of you. Instead of pretending you understand, you ask the hard question that exposes the gap.
translationLiteral: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / अपरम् (later/recent) / भवतः (your) / जन्म (birth) / परम् (earlier/ancient) / जन्म (birth) / विवस्वतः (of the Sun) / कथम् (how) / एतत् (this) / विजानीयाम् (may I understand) / त्वम् (you) / आदौ (at the beginning) / प्रोक्तवान् (had spoken) / इति (thus)
insight: It shifts the mind from passive acceptance to careful inquiry before belief.
